evidence: get_dhcp_config (R2): pool R2_LAN: network 172.16.0.0/24 (pool exists but full range excluded)
R2 interface present on LAN: get_running_config (R2): GigabitEthernet0/0/0 address 172.16.0.1/24

Inter-router control plane is healthy: get_ospf outputs show OSPF neighbors full between routers (e.g. R2 Serial0/1/0 neighbor 1.1.1.1 @10.0.0.1 (full)); R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/0 shows interface_state "DR" — indicates LAN is up and being advertised

missing_info: Is 172.16.0.12 supposed to be assigned via DHCP or statically configured?
ARP/ND entry for 172.16.0.12 on R2 (show ip arp 172.16.0.12) and MAC/port of the host
Interface status and counters for R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/0 (physical/link state, errors)
Ping/traceroute to 172.16.0.12 from R2 itself (to distinguish host-local issue vs. upstream path)

plan_history.intent_description: R2's DHCP excluded-address range covers entire 172.16.0.0/24, preventing DHCP assignment of 172.16.0.12 and leaving the host unreachable; restore DHCP assignment for the 172.16.0.0/24 LAN so the host can obtain 172.16.0.12 (or allow its static address).
plan_history.problem: R2 DHCP config excludes the full 172.16.0.0/24 range (172.16.0.1-172.16.0.254), preventing clients on that LAN (including 172.16.0.12) from receiving addresses.
plan_history.fix_summary: Remove the DHCP excluded-address range that omits the entire 172.16.0.0/24 network from R2's DHCP configuration so DHCP clients on that LAN can be assigned addresses.

remaining_issues: Reachability of host 172.16.0.12 is still unverified — previous pings showed 0/25 but no follow-up host-level checks were performed after the DHCP change.
Need to confirm whether the host actually obtained an IP (DHCP lease) or is statically configured; if DHCP, verify DHCP bindings on R2 and the host's lease state.
